Optimism - Intraday post (12/Dec)

Weekly post:

FOMC-Week, Positioning breakdown - Weekly post (08-12/Dec)

In the weekly post I wrote: “on Friday the optimism is expected to stabilize” Here we are. The weekly post originally expected a bigger profit taking into Thursday, but the base case was a hawkish cut. The dovishness mitigated the profit taking, and optimism came back earlier than originally expected. Still, intraday levels worked out nice.

Structurally, as long as SPX closes above 6800, the rebound is stable.

Let’s look at the intraday positioning…

INTRADAY POST Coding today’s positioning:

The speed profile is again very supportive, but this time we have a strong upside vacuum too. Customers are selling downside vol towards the 6829.95/6800 area, and heavily bidding up volatility towards 6937.49The line in the sand is 6894.98

The centroid of the profile is at 6912.86 - 6929.58,very significant zone, and the upper bound is sticky during the last 2 hours of the session.

Upside pivot is at 6927.60, with target of 6937.49then a reversion back to 6895/6900 is the likeliest scenarioand the second most likely is a pin to 6930, if the centroid can be held. Market has a slight bet on this with local call spread.

Downside pivot is at 6881.49with target of 6829.95 (but I would mark the 6840/50 zone too)This is again a reversion opportunity, back to the “line in the sand”, or to the downside pivot.

As volatility is expected to catch up into either direction, this profile is a good opportunity to fly trades and long vol reversion trades. To the upside, be careful, because daily vol of vol is under pressure, so the convexity is tempered. Market’s main bet is the upside scenario, like 57.39% more chance for it, than downside scenario, however neither of them is significant.

Left-tail would happen only, if very bad policy news come about tariffs, taking us back to 6750.Low probability but never zero.

OptionsDepth Heatmap
